purpose: Every persistent artifact: owner, mechanism, schema, lifecycle, serialization, migration, backup, restore, mutation authority, readers, writers.
provenance: Cycle-1 inventory + Cycle-2 mutation matrix + Cycle-3 side-effect graph + Cycle-5 AST census (74 Ledger.transaction sites, 1 mkdtemp, 2 rmtree, lock sites). Cross-validated: the Cycle-5 AST pass independently reproduced Cycle-3's counts exactly.

the_five_mutable_surfaces.A_B_exclusion: NONE (COUP-01). This is the single most important concurrency fact in the system.

datastores.mechanism: SQLite, WAL, synchronous=FULL, chmod 0o600 after every write
datastores.write_granularity: FULL-DOCUMENT REPLACE — every row of every map, every save (ledger_sqlite.py:64-71). There is no per-row update.
datastores.serialization: pydantic model_dump; validators run at CONSTRUCTION ONLY (Ledger.load). model_copy(update=) and setattr BOTH bypass every validator on every model (INV-01b, executed against pydantic 2.13.4).
datastores.mutation_authority: 74 Ledger.transaction sites (AST). Top: studio/actions.py (25), cli.py (17), studio/actions_approve.py (7), studio/actions_run.py (7), post/run.py (5).

migration.legible_history: v7 ADDS selection_facts; v11 DROPS selections — the P11 casting teardown is readable in the migration chain itself

datastores.forward_compat: every ledger model is pydantic extra='ignore' (INV-19, SHIM-005) — an OLDER binary parsing a NEWER ledger drops unknown keys rather than crashing. Switching any ledger model to extra='forbid' turns a forward-rolled ledger into a hard ControlFileError.
datastores.restore: Ledger.restore_snapshot (ledger.py:551) — takes _file_lock(ledger.lock), which EXCLUDES NOTHING, then os.replace's the db file
datastores.restore_verdict: *** RC-4 / C4-F2a — CRITICAL. A concurrent writer's commit() SUCCEEDS and its data is silently discarded, AND its deferred media unlinks PROCEED, deleting real .mp4 files whose rows the restore brings back. PROVEN BY EXECUTION (Cycles 2 and 4). The correct pattern sits SIX LINES ABOVE in the same class. ***
datastores.crash_recovery: WAL rolls back an open transaction; the flock is kernel-released on process death. kill -9 self-heals with no manual rm.
datastores.silent_corruption_class: ONE — a terminal-state write with no public_url SAVES CLEANLY and BRICKS THE NEXT Ledger.load with a ControlFileError, taking down the daemon and every Studio page at once. Currently LATENT (no path produces the row); the property survives via FOUR independent manual call-site guards, NOT via the type (INV-01).

control_files.load_leniency: PER-ROW (MOL-79) — one malformed row is SKIPPED into skipped_rows, never crashes the registry. A wrong TOP-LEVEL shape still fails loud.
control_files.validation_gap: backends[platform] is dict[str,str], UNVALIDATED at load. Accounts.validate() checks the integration/backend PAIRING, never the VALUE. RC-3.

gate_queue.mechanism: *** THE ONLY QUEUE IN THE SYSTEM IS A FILESYSTEM QUEUE. *** No broker, no in-process queue. Correlated by a stamped request_id.
gate_queue.NON_atomic_write: *** bump_attempts (agentstep.py:147) uses a bare write_text. *** Its TWO SIBLINGS IN THE SAME FILE are atomic. A torn attempts.json hits `except: n=0` and SILENTLY RESETS the 3-attempt ceiling, making the bounded escalation UNBOUNDED. C3-F6.

kinds.intro_match: *** STRUCTURALLY UNANSWERABLE *** — written (intro_match.py:108) but ABSENT from responder._SCHEMA, which answer_pending iterates. INV-04. Dormant behind FANOPS_INTRO_TEASE (DEFAULT OFF).

filesystem_artifacts.constraint: *** the .mp4 SUFFIX IS REQUIRED — ffmpeg picks the muxer by EXTENSION (COUP-07 / MOL-78) ***
filesystem_artifacts.cleanup: cascade unlink, DEFERRED until AFTER commit (ledger.py:487-488) — a rolled-back txn never deletes a file it did not drop. *** This safety property INVERTS in a restore race: the txn commits into an ORPHANED inode, so it is never rolled back, so the unlinks PROCEED. C4-F2a. ***

filesystem_artifacts.primitive: O_CREAT|O_WRONLY|O_TRUNC 0o600 — created 0600 ATOMICALLY, no world-readable window

filesystem_artifacts.contract: NEVER raises (audit.py:46). CONTRACT-CORRECT: 'the log is a tail-and-grep surface, not load-bearing for correctness.' But it is written AFTER the txn commits, so a crash between commit and audit LOSES THE AUDIT LINE WHILE KEEPING THE STATE CHANGE. Audit is a RECORD, not a JOURNAL. C3-F13.

filesystem_artifacts.mitigation: STRUCTURAL — the FLOCK is the authority and is kernel-released on death, so the LEASE self-heals; only an advisory breadcrumb is lost. C3-F7.

filesystem_artifacts.cleanup: *** NEVER. The ONLY mkdtemp in src/fanops (AST-confirmed), with NO rmtree anywhere in the tree. RC-10. ***
filesystem_artifacts.durable_pointer_into_it: Post.media_urls (C4-F5) — written by the Studio oversize-retry path INSIDE a committed transaction. SELF-HEALS: media_path_for_post falls through to clip.path and the next shrink re-points it (PROVEN, EXP-C4-4).

caches.invalidation: *** NONE. *** If the hosted URL expires, the dead CDN URL is reused FOREVER. C3-F11. This FALSIFIES the claim that every cache has an invalidation rule.

caches.key: post/media.py:58 — a Postiz URL is identified BY IT CONTAINING A LITERAL '|'
caches.verdict: COUP-17 — an undocumented URL-shape convention. A Postiz URL format change that drops the '|' silently re-uploads every clip.

fingerprints.rule: geom = bool(track) or (focus is not None and len(focus) > 2) (clip.py:633). content_type + _REFRAME_GEOM_V (=4) are hashed ONLY when geom is TRUE — so centred clips keep their historic fingerprint and never needlessly re-render.
SHA-1, usedforsecurity=False, \x00-joined, [:12]. Builtin hash() BANNED (PEP 456). INV-16.

consistency_mechanisms.two_phase_commit: NONE ANYWHERE, and none is needed — except across the publish boundary, where claim-before-network + needs_reconcile is the (correct) substitute.
consistency_mechanisms.the_publish_durability_point: the CLAIM (queued->submitting) is COMMITTED BEFORE ANY NETWORK I/O (run.py:264-272). A crash mid-network leaves `submitting`, which publish_due NEVER re-drives. That is CORRECT (F11). The defect is that reconcile — the only READER of `submitting` — cannot always terminate it.
consistency_mechanisms.deferred_unlink: cascade .mp4 unlinks drain AFTER _save_unlocked (ledger.py:487-488). Correct against a rollback; INVERTS against a restore race (C4-F2a).
